channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Credit Bank sues Moses Kuria and associated firms over Sh72 million unpaid loan linked to Bomet University projects
item.description: Former Kenyan Cabinet Secretary Moses Kuria, together with associated companies and co-directors, is facing a commercial court case over an alleged unpaid loan amounting to more than Ksh72 million. Th...

item.title: Concerns over market fairness and trading activity linked to Donald Trump’s presidency
item.description: The article examines concerns raised by market commentators and analysts regarding stock trading activity and potential financial gains linked to decisions and announcements made during Donald Trump’s...

item.title: PM Shehbaz begins four-day official visit to China for talks on bilateral cooperation and CPEC
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has arrived in China on a four-day official visit, marking an important phase in Pakistan-China relations as both countries celebrate the 75th anniversary of diplomatic t...

item.title: Zwane upbeat as Sundowns prepare for CAF Champions League final second leg against AS FAR
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns captain Themba Zwane has expressed confidence and determination as the team prepares for the decisive second leg of the CAF Champions League final against AS FAR in Rabat. Sundowns c...

item.title: Tanzania Appoints Rio Ferdinand to Promote Tourism Globally
item.description: Former Manchester United defender Rio Ferdinand has officially been appointed as Tanzania’s new tourism ambassador in a move aimed at strengthening the country’s international tourism promotion effort...

item.title: Allegations Of Illegal Passport Fees Trigger Internal Crisis And Staff Transfers In Enugu Immigration Command
item.description: Serious controversy don break out inside the Enugu State Command of the Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) after allegations of illegal passport charges and sudden mass transfer of officers. According ...

item.title: Marion Michael, German Film Star Who Rose to Fame with 'Liane, Jungle Goddess' in the 1950s
item.description: Marion Michael (1940–2007) was a German actress who became an unexpected star in the mid-1950s after achieving major fame at just 15 years old. Her breakthrough role came in the 1956 adventure film 'L...

item.title: EEDC System Upgrade Disrupts Electricity Payments and Meter Recharge Across South-east Nigeria
item.description: Residents across South-east Nigeria are currently experiencing temporary electricity service disruption after the Enugu Electricity Distribution Company (EEDC) announced the shutdown of its vending pl...

item.title: Deep-Sea Golden Orb Identified as Remains of Rare Anemone
item.description: A mysterious golden orb discovered on the seafloor of the Gulf of Alaska during a NOAA Ocean Exploration expedition has finally been identified after years of scientific analysis. Initially retrieved ...

item.title: Eco India examines environmental costs and trade-offs of rapid urbanisation
item.description: This article refers to an episode of Scroll.in’s weekly series Eco India, which focuses on environmental issues and sustainability challenges. Episode 322 discusses the ecological trade-offs associate...

item.title: Understanding “himpathy” and how society shields men accused of gender-based violence
item.description: The article explains the concept of “himpathy”, a term used to describe the excessive or unwarranted sympathy often extended to men accused of sexual misconduct, gender-based violence, or related abus...

item.title: TFS strengthens Tanzania’s beekeeping value chain through value addition and marketing initiatives
item.description: Tanzania is making strong efforts to improve its beekeeping sector by focusing on value addition, better marketing, and sustainable production practices through the Tanzania Forest Services Agency (TF...

item.title: Cayetano seeks impartial probe into Senate shooting as authorities find no attack
item.description: Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano has called for an independent and impartial investigation into the shooting incident that occurred inside the Senate premises on May 13. Speaking via Facebook Live...

item.title: Ontario and Quebec rural groups raise environmental and property concerns over proposed Alto high-speed rail project
item.description: Farmers, environmental organizations, snowmobiling groups and rural residents are voicing concerns about the proposed Alto high-speed rail network planned to connect Toronto and Quebec City. The feder...

item.title: Piapot First Nation and volunteers support North Central neighbourhood cleanup in Regina
item.description: Members of Piapot First Nation joined local community volunteers in Regina’s North Central neighbourhood to take part in a coordinated cleanup effort aimed at improving the appearance and safety of th...

item.title: Federal High Court announces passing of Justice Mohammed Nasir Yunusa, honours judicial service and integrity
item.description: The Federal High Court has announced the death of Hon. Justice Mohammed Nasir Yunusa, who served in the Kano Judicial Division. According to an official statement released by the court’s Director of I...

item.title: Amaechi Disputes Tinubu’s Claim of Being Key to Buhari’s 2015 Election Victory
item.description: Former Minister of Transportation, Rotimi Amaechi, don push back against President Bola Tinubu’s repeated claim say him played the main role in making late Muhammadu Buhari win the 2015 presidential e...

item.title: US, Iran continue peace negotiations despite major disagreements over uranium and Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Nagpapatuloy ang diplomatic talks sa pagitan ng United States at Iran habang sinusubukan ng magkabilang panig na mapanatili ang marupok na ceasefire matapos ang ilang linggo ng digmaan sa Middle East....

item.title: Montreal tribunal orders landlord to pay $62,000 to tenant with cancer over bad faith eviction
item.description: A tribunal in Montreal has ruled that a landlord must pay $62,000 in compensation to a tenant who is battling cancer and to the tenant’s spouse after finding that the eviction was carried out in bad f...

item.title: Rod Stewart’s Career and Style Evolution in the 1980s
item.description: The article reviews Rod Stewart’s transformation during the 1980s, a decade in which the British singer successfully adapted to changing musical trends while facing both commercial success and critica...
